title: Shopping in China Tips

sections.heading: Getting Started
sections.content: Ensure you have mobile payment apps like Alipay or WeChat Pay set up for seamless transactions. Bargaining is expected in markets and small shops but not in malls or supermarkets. Always check your items and receipt before leaving the store to avoid issues.

sections.heading: Key Tips
sections.content: Tip A: Learn a few basic Mandarin phrases for numbers and common shopping terms. Tip B: Compare prices at multiple vendors, especially for souvenirs. Tip C: Be aware of counterfeit goods and buy from reputable stores.

sections.heading: What to Remember
sections.content: Remember 1: Cash is still useful in smaller towns or with street vendors. Remember 2: Keep your receipts for any potential returns or customs checks. Remember 3: Look for 'Tax-Free' signs in larger stores to claim VAT refunds if eligible.

keyTakeaways: Set up mobile payments for convenience
Bargain in markets, not in fixed-price stores
Always verify items and receipts before leaving
